AFRICAN WAR MEMORIAL FUND 37 of 1954
31 of 1956
18 of 1961
An Act to make new provision for the administration of the African War Memorial Statutory Instrument
Fund; and to provide for matters incidental to and connected therewith. 53 of 1965
[14th December, 1954]

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the African War Memorial Fund Act. Short title
sections.text: This Act may be cited as the African War Memorial Fund Act. Short title

sections.heading: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires- Interpretation
sections.text:
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires- Interpretation
"the Board" means the African War Memorial Fund Board of Trustees established
under section three;
"the Fund" means the African War Memorial Fund.

sections.heading: (1) For the purposes of managing the Fund and for the other purposes of this Establishment of
sections.text:
(1) For the purposes of managing the Fund and for the other purposes of this Establishment of
Act, there shall be established a Board of Trustees which shall be a body corporate with Board of Trustees
the name of "The African War Memorial Fund Board of Trustees", with perpetual
succession and power to acquire and hold and sell or otherwise dispose of land.
(2) The Fund shall consist of-
(a) the moneys and investments forming part of the Fund at the
commencement of this Act;
(b) such other sums as may, from time to time, be credited to the Fund by the
Government or any person for the purposes of this Act.
(As amended by No. 31 of 1956 and No. 18 of 1961)

sections.heading: (1) The Board shall consist of nine members appointed from time to time by the Constitution of Board
sections.text:
(1) The Board shall consist of nine members appointed from time to time by the Constitution of Board
Minister. At least five of the members shall be ex-servicemen.
(2) Any member of the Board (hereinafter referred to as "a trustee") shall remain a
trustee unless and until-
(a) he becomes bankrupt; or
(b) he leaves Zambia and remains out of it for a continuous period of more
than twelve calendar months; or
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(c) his period of office expires; or
(d) he resigns from the Board having given two months' notice of his intention
so to do to the Minister; or
(e) he is dismissed by the Minister.
(3) The trustees shall hold office for such period as the Minister may direct.
(4) If any vacancy occurs by death, resignation, effluxion of time, or otherwise in the
office of any trustee, the vacancy shall be filled by appointment by the Minister who may
also appoint an alternate to any trustee who is, by reason of absence from Zambia, illness
or other cause, unable for the time being to serve as a trustee.
(5) Any person ceasing to be a trustee shall be eligible for reappointment.
(6) The powers of the Board may be exercised notwithstanding any vacancy in its
number.
(7) The Board may appoint one of its members to be the chairman of the Board,
who, if present, shall preside at all its meetings. In the absence of the chairman, the
trustees present at a meeting shall elect one of their number to preside for the time being
in his place.
(8) A quorum of the Board shall consist of five trustees for the purposes of any
meeting.
(As amended by No. 31 of 1956, No. 18 of 1961 and S.I. No. 53 of 1965)

sections.heading: The Board may, with the approval of the Minister, by statutory instrument, make Proceedings of Board
sections.text:
The Board may, with the approval of the Minister, by statutory instrument, make Proceedings of Board
rules for regulating the proceedings of the Board.
(As amended by S.I. No. 53 of 1965)

sections.heading: The Board may take such steps as it considers necessary to provide for the Management of
sections.text:
The Board may take such steps as it considers necessary to provide for the Management of
management of any building erected on land which is vested in the Board or of any buildings
building erected with funds allocated by the Board under the provisions of this Act.
(No. 18 of 1961)
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: The Board shall have the general control and management of the Fund and for Powers and duties of
sections.text:
The Board shall have the general control and management of the Fund and for Powers and duties of
that purpose shall hold the moneys and investments forming part of the Fund and may- Board
(a) permit the moneys referred to to remain in the present state of investment
or at its absolute discretion sell, call in or convert into money the same or
any part thereof; and
(i) s ubject to the provisions of section eight, expend the net proceeds
or any part thereof thereby produced for all or any of the purposes
referred to in section eight; or
(ii) invest the net moneys or any part thereof thereby produced in
accordance with the provisions of section ten;
(b) expend the twenty thousand kwacha previously allocated for the provision
of an Old Soldiers' Home for elderly indigent African ex-servicemen on the
provision of one or more such homes as may be considered desirable by
the Board:
Provided that, with the consent of the Minister, such allocation may be
increased if the Board considers the increase necessary;
(c) hold the moneys allocated for the Old Soldiers' Home in investment until
such a home is required, meanwhile devoting the income thereof-
(i) t o meet any costs incidental to the management of buildings under
section six;
(ii) to the provision of bursaries under the provisions of section eight.
(As amended by No. 31 of 1956, No. 18 of 1961 and S.I. No. 53 of 1965)

sections.heading: (1) The Board shall hold the balance of the Fund remaining, after any moneys Bursaries and grants
sections.text:
(1) The Board shall hold the balance of the Fund remaining, after any moneys Bursaries and grants
set aside under paragraph (b) of section seven have been deducted, for the purposes-
(a) of providing bursaries for African ex-servicemen and the sons, daughters or
other dependants of African ex-servicemen, taking into consideration any
recommendations made by the African Bursaries Committee or such other
body as the Minister may from time to time appoint, and paying such other
sums as, in the opinion of the Board, may be necessary for the educational
advancement of any such African within or outside Zambia;
(b) of providing from time to time such sums as the Board may think fit for
grants to African ex-servicemen or their families as may to the Board
appear justified;
(c) of providing funds for any purpose whatsoever, whether similar to the
foregoing or not, which, in the opinion of the Board, is for the benefit of
African ex-servicemen or their families, whether severally or collectively.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(2) If at any time, in the opinion of the Board, the reasonable requirements of all
applicants for assistance under subsection (1) have been met, the Board may provide
assistance of a nature similar to that mentioned in paragraph (a) of subsection (1) to
Africans other than those mentioned therein.
(3) The Board may sell, call in or convert into money any investment made under
the provisions of sub-paragraph (ii) of paragraph (a) of section seven and expend the net
proceeds thereby produced for all or any of the purposes referred to in subsections (1)
and (2):
Provided that the amount so expended, together with any amount expended under
the provisions of sub-paragraph (i) of paragraph (a) of section seven, shall not exceed in
total an amount of fifty thousand kwacha unless the Minister shall have given his consent
to any expenditure in excess of such amount.
(As amended by No. 31 of 1956, No. 18 of 1961 and S.I. No. 53 of 1965)

sections.heading: The Board shall have absolute discretion in deciding whether, under the Discretion of Board
sections.text:
The Board shall have absolute discretion in deciding whether, under the Discretion of Board
provisions of section eight, any person should or should not receive assistance from the
Board.

sections.heading: Any moneys liable to be invested under the terms of this Act may be invested Investment of moneys
sections.text:
Any moneys liable to be invested under the terms of this Act may be invested Investment of moneys
in or upon any of the investments mentioned in the Schedule or may be placed on fixed
deposit with the Standard Bank Limited or Barclays Bank D.C.O. or any other Bank
operating within Zambia which may be approved by the Minister, and the Board may, from
time to time at its absolute discretion, vary or transpose any such investments for or into
such authorised investments.
(As amended by S.I. No. 53 of 1965)

sections.heading: All moneys and investments, from time to time forming part of the Fund, shall Investment of funds
sections.text:
All moneys and investments, from time to time forming part of the Fund, shall Investment of funds
be held and invested in the name of the Board.

sections.heading: The Board shall have the power to appoint a secretary or clerk to assist in its Staff and expenses
sections.text:
The Board shall have the power to appoint a secretary or clerk to assist in its Staff and expenses
work and to record the minutes of its meetings, and to pay him reasonable remuneration
and expenses from the Fund, and to dismiss him if and when necessary. It shall be lawful
for the secretary or clerk to make such small disbursements of cash from the Fund as may
from time to time be reasonably necessary for the conduct of the business and
correspondence of the Board.

sections.heading: In case the trustees shall not be unanimous upon any matter arising in or out of Opinion of majority of
sections.text:
In case the trustees shall not be unanimous upon any matter arising in or out of Opinion of majority of
the business of the Board, the decision of the majority of the trustees present and voting, the trustees to be
in the case of a matter to be decided at any meeting or in any other case, the decision of binding on the minority
the majority of all the trustees shall bind the dissenting minority, and the acts of the
majority in execution of the powers hereby given to and reposed in the Board shall be as
valid as if they had acted in such matter unanimously.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: The Trust Deed numbered Miscellaneous 1044 in the Registry of Lands and Annulment of Trust
sections.text:
The Trust Deed numbered Miscellaneous 1044 in the Registry of Lands and Annulment of Trust
Deeds, under the authority of which the Fund has hitherto been administered, is hereby Deed and validation of
annulled: acts
Provided that any act or thing validly and properly done under the provisions of the
said deed shall be deemed to have been done under the provisions of this Act.
SCHEDULE
(Section 10)
INVESTMENT OF MONEYS

sections.heading: Any of the Parliamentary stocks or public funds or Government securities of the United
sections.text:
Any of the Parliamentary stocks or public funds or Government securities of the United
Kingdom, the Republic of Zambia or any British Colony.

sections.heading: Any securities the interest of which is for the time being guaranteed by the Parliament of the
sections.text:
Any securities the interest of which is for the time being guaranteed by the Parliament of the
United Kingdom.

sections.heading: Real or heritable securities within the Republic of Zambia.
sections.text: Real or heritable securities within the Republic of Zambia.

sections.heading: Debentures, or debenture stock or shares of any railway or other company a fixed or minimum
sections.text:
Debentures, or debenture stock or shares of any railway or other company a fixed or minimum
rate of interest or dividend on which is guaranteed by the Parliament of the United Kingdom or by the
Government of the Republic of Zambia or any British Colony.

sections.heading: Stock of the Bank of England.
sections.text: Stock of the Bank of England.

sections.heading: Stocks, funds or securities authorised under the Colonial Stock Act, 1900, of the United
sections.text:
Stocks, funds or securities authorised under the Colonial Stock Act, 1900, of the United
Kingdom, or any Act extending the same, but subject to any restriction thereby imposed.
(As amended by No. 18 of 1961 and S.I. No. 53 of 1965)
